pull_request.title: feat: reinstate Java SWIG bindings with array support
pull_request.body:
## Summary

- Remove `#ifndef SWIGJAVA` guard that excluded `carrays.i` and `%array_class` from Java bindings, preventing creation of `double *` and `int *` arrays
- Java bindings now have `doubleArray` and `intArray` classes with `new`/`delete`/`getitem`/`setitem`/`cast` methods, making vector functions (spectrum, autocorrelation, mfcc, etc.) usable
- Add `java` target to `swig/Makefile` with platform-specific library extension (`.dylib` on macOS, `.so` on Linux)

Closes #12

## Test plan

- [x] `make && make check` passes (375 assertions, 61 test cases)
- [x] `make -C swig java` generates 31 Java classes and compiles cleanly
- [x] Runtime smoke test: `doubleArray`/`intArray` create, read, and write correctly via JNI
- [ ] Verify on Linux

changed_files.patch:
@@ -9,34 +9,68 @@ on:
 jobs:
   build-and-test:
     strategy:
+      fail-fast: false
       matrix:
-        os: [ubuntu-latest, windows-latest]
+        os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest]
     runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
 
     steps:
       - uses: actions/checkout@v4
 
+      - name: Setup Java
+        uses: actions/setup-java@v4
+        with:
+          distribution: temurin
+          java-version: 17
+
       - name: Setup MSYS2 (Windows)
         if: runner.os == 'Windows'
         uses: msys2/setup-msys2@v2
         with:
           msystem: MINGW64
-          install: mingw-w64-x86_64-gcc make
+          install: mingw-w64-x86_64-gcc mingw-w64-x86_64-python mingw-w64-x86_64-swig make
 
-      - name: Build (Linux)
+      - name: Install SWIG (Linux)
         if: runner.os == 'Linux'
-        run: make -C src
+        run: sudo apt-get install -y swig
 
-      - name: Build (Windows)
+      - name: Install SWIG (macOS)
+        if: runner.os == 'macOS'
+        run: brew install swig
+
+      - name: Build and test (Linux)
+        if: runner.os == 'Linux'
+        run: |
+          make
+          make check
+
+      - name: Build and test (macOS)
+        if: runner.os == 'macOS'
+        run: |
+          make
+          make check
+
+      - name: Build and test (Windows)
         if: runner.os == 'Windows'
         shell: msys2 {0}
-        run: make -C src
+        run: |
+          make
+          make check
 
-      - name: Test (Linux)
+      - name: SWIG bindings (Linux)
         if: runner.os == 'Linux'
-        run: make check
+        run: make -C swig check
+        env:
+          JAVA_HOME: ${{ env.JAVA_HOME }}
+          PYTHON: python3
+
+      - name: SWIG bindings (macOS)
+        if: runner.os == 'macOS'
+        run: make -C swig check
+        env:
+          JAVA_HOME: ${{ env.JAVA_HOME }}
 
-      - name: Test (Windows)
+      - name: SWIG bindings (Windows)
         if: runner.os == 'Windows'
         shell: msys2 {0}
-        run: make check
+        run: PYTHON=python make -C swig check-python

@@ -2,36 +2,71 @@ NAME = xtract
 
 OS := $(shell uname)
 
-# Assume that since we're building the python bindings, we have a python installed!
-INCLUDEPY = $(shell python -m sysconfig | grep -w INCLUDEPY | awk '{print $$3}')
+PYTHON ?= python3
+INCLUDEPY = $(shell $(PYTHON) -m sysconfig | grep -w INCLUDEPY | awk '{print $$3}')
+PYTHON_LDFLAGS = $(shell $(PYTHON)-config --ldflags --embed 2>/dev/null || echo "")
+
+PYTHON_EXT_SUFFIX = $(shell $(PYTHON) -c "import sysconfig; print(sysconfig.get_config_var('EXT_SUFFIX') or '.so')" 2>/dev/null)
 
 ifeq ($(OS), Darwin)
     CFLAGS=-g -c
     LDFLAGS=-bundle -flat_namespace -undefined suppress
     LIBS=-framework Accelerate
+    JAVA_LIB_SUFFIX=dylib
+    LIBXTRACT=../src/lib$(NAME).a
+    LINK_LIB=$(LIBXTRACT)
 else
     CFLAGS=-g -c -fPIC
     LDFLAGS=-shared
-    LIBS=
+    LIBS=-lm
+    JAVA_LIB_SUFFIX=so
+    LIBXTRACT=$(wildcard ../src/lib$(NAME).a ../src/lib$(NAME).lib)
+    LINK_LIB=-Wl,--whole-archive $(LIBXTRACT) -Wl,--no-whole-archive
 endif
 
-.PHONY: python
+.PHONY: python java check check-python check-java
 
 python:
 	@swig -I../include -python $(NAME).i
 	@$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(NAME)_wrap.c -o $(NAME)_wrap.o -I$(INCLUDEPY) -I../include
-	@$(CC) $(LDFLAGS) ../src/lib$(NAME).a $(NAME)_wrap.o -o _$(NAME).so $(LIBS)
+	@$(CC) $(LDFLAGS) $(LINK_LIB) $(NAME)_wrap.o -o _$(NAME)$(PYTHON_EXT_SUFFIX) $(LIBS) $(PYTHON_LDFLAGS)
+
+java:
+ifndef JAVA_HOME
+	$(error JAVA_HOME is not set. Set it to your JDK installation directory)
+endif
+	@mkdir -p java
+	@swig -I../include -java -package $(NAME) -outdir java $(NAME).i
+	@$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(NAME)_wrap.c -o $(NAME)_wrap.o -I$(JAVA_HOME)/include -I$(wildcard $(JAVA_HOME)/include/darwin $(JAVA_HOME)/include/linux $(JAVA_HOME)/include/win32) -I../include
+	@$(CC) $(LDFLAGS) $(LINK_LIB) $(NAME)_wrap.o -o libj$(NAME).$(JAVA_LIB_SUFFIX) $(LIBS)
+	@javac java/*.java
+
+check: check-python check-java
+
+check-python: python
+	@echo "Running Python bindings test..."
+	@PYTHONPATH=. $(PYTHON) test.py
+
+check-java: java
+	@echo "Running Java bindings test..."
+	@mkdir -p $(NAME)
+	@cp java/*.class $(NAME)/
+	@javac -cp . test.java
+	@java -Djava.library.path=. -cp . test
+	@$(RM) -r $(NAME) test.class
 
 install: python
-	@SITE_DIR=$$(python -c "import site; print(site.getsitepackages()[0])") && \
+	@SITE_DIR=$$($(PYTHON) -c "import site; print(site.getsitepackages()[0])") && \
 	install -d "$$SITE_DIR" && \
-	install _$(NAME).so "$$SITE_DIR" && \
+	install _$(NAME)$(PYTHON_EXT_SUFFIX) "$$SITE_DIR" && \
 	install -m 644 $(NAME).py "$$SITE_DIR"
 
 clean:
 	@$(RM) *.o
 	@$(RM) *.pyc
 	@$(RM) *.so
+	@$(RM) *.dylib
 	@$(RM) $(NAME)_wrap.c
 	@$(RM) $(NAME).py
+	@$(RM) -r java

@@ -1,9 +1,12 @@
-#!/usr/bin/python
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+
+import sys
 
 try:
     import xtract
-except ImportError:
-    print('Failed to load the library "xtract"')
+except ImportError as e:
+    print('Failed to load the library "xtract": ' + str(e))
+    sys.exit(1)
 
 print('\nRunning libxtract Python bindings test...\n')

commit_summaries.message:
feat: reinstate Java SWIG bindings with array support

Remove the #ifndef SWIGJAVA guard that excluded carrays.i and
%array_class from Java, preventing creation of double* and int*
arrays. Java bindings now have doubleArray and intArray classes
with new/delete/getitem/setitem/cast methods, making vector
functions usable.

Add a java target to swig/Makefile that generates and compiles
the Java bindings with correct platform-specific library extension.

Verified: SWIG generates 31 Java classes, javac compiles them,
and doubleArray/intArray work correctly at runtime via JNI.

Closes #12

fix: require JAVA_HOME instead of auto-detecting JDK path

The java -XshowSettings parsing was brittle and format-dependent.
Require JAVA_HOME to be set explicitly, which is standard practice
for Java projects.

feat: add Java test mirroring test.py

Tests mean, variance, and spectrum extraction using doubleArray
to verify Java SWIG bindings work end-to-end. Based on the
original test.java from 2012, updated for double precision and
current API.

fix: use xtract package name for Java bindings

Match the convention used by Python bindings (import xtract).

fix: rename Java native library to jxtract

Match the original naming convention. Library is now libjxtract.dylib
(macOS) / libjxtract.so (Linux).

feat: add SWIG binding tests to CI and swig/Makefile check target

Add make check target to swig/Makefile that builds and runs both
Python and Java binding tests. Use PYTHON variable (default python3)
for portability.

Update CI workflow:
- Add macOS to the build matrix
- Install SWIG on all platforms
- Run swig check (Python + Java) on Linux and macOS
- Run swig check-python on Windows (Java/JNI under MSYS2 is fragile)

fix: CI failures — add -lm on Linux, fix MSYS2 packages, disable fail-fast

- Link _xtract.so with -lm on Linux (math functions from libxtract.a)
- Fix MSYS2 package names to use mingw-w64-x86_64- prefix
- Use python (not python3) under MSYS2
- Disable fail-fast so all platforms report independently
- Exit test.py on import failure instead of continuing with undefined xtract
- Use python3 as default PYTHON, configurable via env var

fix: CI failures — whole-archive linking on Linux, random() portability

- Use --whole-archive when linking libxtract.a into SWIG shared
  libraries on Linux, so all symbols (including xtract_free_window)
  are included. Static archive linking otherwise only pulls in
  object files that resolve existing undefined symbols.
- Replace POSIX random() with portable rand() in simpletest.cpp
  to fix Windows/MinGW build.

fix: Windows CI — find libxtract.lib and simplify link commands

On Windows/MSYS2 the static library is libxtract.lib not libxtract.a.
Use wildcard to find whichever exists. Also consolidate the
platform-specific link logic into LINK_LIB variable to avoid
repeated ifeq blocks.

fix: skip SWIG bindings on Windows CI

MSYS2 Python extensions require explicit linking against libpython
which needs additional setup. The core library and unit tests still
run on Windows; SWIG bindings are tested on Linux and macOS.

Revert "fix: skip SWIG bindings on Windows CI"

This reverts commit 5771ad5d28dfdb7164dd11ac8fdbfd0621215474.

fix: link Python extensions against libpython on Windows

On Windows/MinGW, all symbols must be resolved at link time.
Add $(PYTHON)-config --ldflags --embed to the Python extension
link step to pick up -lpython3.x on platforms that require it.
Falls back to empty string if python-config is not available.

fix: use correct Python extension suffix on Windows (.pyd not .so)

Query Python's EXT_SUFFIX via sysconfig to get the platform-correct
extension module filename. On Windows/MSYS2 this produces .pyd,
on Linux .cpython-3xx-linux-gnu.so, on macOS .cpython-3xx-darwin.so.
